commit.message:
feat(fuzzer): switch to pc machine, add 4 new op types, expand attack surface

- Change DEFAULT_MACHINE from q35 to pc (i440fx): enables PCI hotplugging
  so device_add/device_del (ops 2/3) now succeed and exercise real QEMU
  device lifecycle code instead of always failing with a bus error
- Add ich9-usb-uhci1 USB controller to base QEMU command so USB input
  devices (usb-mouse, usb-kbd, usb-tablet) can be hotplugged
- Replace non-hotplug-capable DEVICE_DRIVERS (pc-testdev, virtio-blk-pci,
  pvpanic-pci) with verified hotplug-capable drivers: ne2k_pci, pcnet,
  pci-testdev, usb-mouse, usb-kbd, usb-tablet (13 drivers total)
- Expand QUERY_COMMANDS: replace query-kvm with query-blockdev and
  query-chardev; expand HMP_COMMANDS to 12 entries including info cpu/mem/
  registers/blockstats
- Add 3 new op types (ops 12-15, total 16 ops, up from 12):
  - Op 12: netdev_add(user) / netdev_del with live-netdev tracking
  - Op 13: chardev-add(null/memory/ringbuf) / chardev-remove with tracking
  - Op 14: blockdev-add(null-co) / blockdev-del with tracking
  - Op 15: extended HMP commands (info cpus/mem/registers/blockstats)
- Add cleanup for live_netdevs, live_chardevs, live_blockdevs before quit
- Update ChunkOpMutator: below(12) → below(16) for new op range
- Add 8 new corpus seeds for ops 12-15 (add and add+del cycles)
- Normalize new dynamic IDs in stdout:
  - 'hnet<N>' in Device 'hnetN' not found errors
  - 'chr<N>'  in Chardev 'chrN' not found/already exists errors
  - ='blk<N>' in node-name='blkN' blockdev error messages
  - thread_id=<N> in info cpus output (OS-assigned thread ID varies per run)
- Exec/sec improved ~21% (19 → 23/worker) from faster hotplug path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: QEMUFuzzer
repository.description: A QEMU Virtual Device Fuzzer.

commit.message:
fix: base station overview status, leaderboard/runner grid deduplication, duplicate SW registration

- Overview tab: seed runner status as NOT_STARTED instead of first checkpoint
  record's status, so runners without a base station entry show correctly
- Leaderboard: deduplicate flat runners array by bib before filtering finishers/
  stillRacing/DNF — prevents runners with both checkpoint and base station records
  from appearing in multiple categories simultaneously
- RaceOverview: same bib deduplication in filteredRunners useMemo
- main.jsx: remove manual /sw.js registration (duplicate of VitePWA's registerSW.js
  injection); keep only the controllerchange reload listener

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement SDF-based text rendering (Phase 1.4c)

Add text rendering package with Signed Distance Field (SDF) support:
- SDF font atlas with pre-baked glyphs (ASCII 0x20-0x7E)
- High-quality scalable text rendering at any size
- O(1) glyph lookup using hash map
- Porter-Duff SrcOver compositing with antialiasing
- Text measurement API for layout calculations

Implementation:
- internal/raster/text/atlas.go: SDF atlas structure and glyph metadata
- internal/raster/text/text.go: Text rendering and SDF sampling
- cmd/gen-atlas/main.go: Build-time atlas generator
- Embedded atlas.bin: 256x256 SDF texture (96 glyphs)

Testing:
- 18 test functions with comprehensive coverage
- 2 benchmark tests for performance validation
- All tests passing

Metrics:
- 379 LoC (production code)
- Max cyclomatic complexity: 9 (threshold: 15)
- Documentation coverage: 98.46%
- Code duplication: 1.6% (threshold: 3%)
- Zero regressions in complexity

Completes PLAN.md Step 12 (Phase 1.4c)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add VS Code Insiders support with configurable editor preference (#289)

## Summary

Add a **VS Code variant** setting that lets users choose between **VS
Code (Stable)** and **VS Code Insiders** across the app.

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Models/ConnectionSettings.cs` | Add `VsCodeVariant` enum
(`Stable`/`Insiders`) + property + validation |
| `Services/CopilotService.cs` | Expose + load `VsCodeVariant` from
settings |
| `Components/Layout/SessionListItem.razor` | Dynamic label ("VS Code" /
"VS Code Insiders") + command (`code` / `code-insiders`) |
| `Services/SettingsRegistry.cs` | Add `ui.editor` CardEnum setting
(desktop only) |
| `Components/Pages/Settings.razor` | Handle `ui.editor` change → sync
to CopilotService |
| `Tests/ConnectionSettingsTests.cs` | 5 new tests: default, enum
values, round-trip, backward compat, invalid value |
| `Tests/SettingsRegistryTests.cs` | 3 new tests: get/set value,
desktop-only visibility |

### Behavior

- **Default**: `Stable` (existing behavior unchanged)
- **Settings → UI → Editor**: Card picker with two options (desktop
only, hidden on mobile)
- **Session menu**: Label and command update based on setting
- **Persistence**: Survives app restarts via `settings.json`
- **Backward compat**: Old settings files without `VsCodeVariant`
default to `Stable`
- **Corrupt guard**: Invalid enum values normalize to `Stable` (same
pattern as `CliSource`)

Closes #251

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: VS Code UX parity — per-phase Working boxes, no Copilot header, custom agents in picker (#120)

## Changes

- **Per-phase Working boxes**: Each \ eport_intent\ event after tools
starts a new collapsible box — matches VS Code's \IChatTask\ model (one
task = one phase)
- **Phase label as header**: The \ eport_intent\ text is shown as the
Working box header in both running (shimmer) and done (✓) states —
removed non-VS-Code 'Finished with N steps' label
- **No Copilot header**: Removed the redundant avatar + 'Copilot' label
that VS Code hides for the GitHub Copilot username
- **Custom agents in picker**: AgentPicker now shows imported/plugin
agents in a 'Custom' section
- **Fix plugin agent E2E test**: Use proven secret-phrase pattern
instead of 'MUST start with' instruction

All 832 integration tests pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Stefan Broenner <stefan.broenner@microsoft.comm>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: office-coding-agent
repository.description: An Office add-in that embeds GitHub Copilot as an AI assistant in Excel, PowerPoint and Word (and other Office hosts). Uses the Copilot SDK.

commit.message:
Add nes-emu-debug skill for Mesen2 runtime debugging (#153)

* Add nes-emu-debug skill for Mesen2 runtime debugging

Adds a new Copilot skill for running NES ROMs headlessly in Mesen2's
test runner and inspecting runtime state. The skill documents:

- Mesen2 --testRunner headless mode (no window, max speed, exit codes)
- Command-line flags including --debug.scriptWindow.allowIoOsAccess=true
  for enabling Lua file I/O
- Lua API: emu.getState() flat table format, emu.read() with NES-specific
  memory types, emu.getScreenBuffer() for 256x240 ARGB screen capture
- Five Lua script templates: CPU state dump, zero page inspection, palette
  dump, nametable dump, and screenshot capture
- Common debugging workflows for display issues and ROM comparison
- Tips for PowerShell/Lua path handling and frame timing

This is the dynamic/runtime counterpart to the existing nes-rom-debug
skill which handles static binary analysis.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update copilot-instructions to reference both debug skills

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: fix zero-page addresses, cross-platform path, here-string

- Fix zero-page variable table to match NESConstants.cs (SCROLL_X is 0x0C
  not 0x0D, RAND_SEED is 0x3C not 0x19, use correct symbol names)
- Make basic invocation example cross-platform by auto-detecting Mesen
  executable path based on OS and build config
- Add note that PowerShell here-string terminator must be at column 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(forge): add Design-First mode with progressive design levels

Implement Design-First collaboration pattern (inspired by Martin Fowler's
article) as a formal DESIGN phase in the Forge agent pipeline.

New DESIGN mode (modes/design.md):
- 4 progressive design levels: Capabilities → Components → Interactions → Contracts
- Each level is a checkpoint requiring user approval before advancing
- Quality Constraints (NFRs) in L1 — architecture-shaping constraints
- Domain Model + Trust Boundaries in L2 — DDD-informed aggregate boundaries
- Failure Modes (required) + State Machines + Event Topology in L3
- Event Contracts + Error Types + Migration Notes in L4
- Complexity-adaptive entry points: T2 contracts-only, T3 components+, T4-T5 full
- Conditional sections activated by triggers, not mandatory ritual

Enhanced existing modes:
- IDEATE: design questions per approach for collaborative refinement
- EXECUTE: Contract-Driven TDD protocol for T3+ tasks with frozen contracts
- VERIFY: Scope Drift Audit + Contract Conformance checks

Updated routing and contracts:
- SKILL.md: DESIGN added to intent classification between IDEATE and PLAN
- MODE_CONTRACTS.md: DESIGN contract + updated transition rules
- ROUTING_RULES.md: design-depth calibration table + routing decision tree
- Build scripts: all 3 platforms (sh, ps1, gpt-ps1) include design mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: product-forge
repository.description: Product Forge Agentic Engineering System

commit.message:
Add comprehensive live event streaming design (Hotstar/Twitch/Netflix)

846-line design document focused on live sports/concert streaming:

Architecture: Camera → Ingest PoP (RTMP/SRT) → GPU Transcoder →
Packager (HLS/DASH/CMAF) → Origin → Origin Shield → Multi-CDN → Viewers

Key sections:
- Capacity estimation: 50M concurrent, 150 Tbps CDN bandwidth,
  25M segment requests/sec, 417K chat messages/sec
- Ingest pipeline: RTMP/SRT, dual-ingest redundancy
- Live transcoding: GPU-accelerated, 5-rendition bitrate ladder
  (240p-1080p), 2s segment duration trade-off analysis
- Packaging: HLS/DASH/CMAF comparison, DRM (Widevine/FairPlay/PlayReady)
- CDN delivery: origin shield, request coalescing for thundering
  herd, multi-CDN strategy, push-based cache population
- ABR playback: throughput vs buffer-based algorithms, player state machine
- DVR seek-back: ring buffer, 4h window, post-event VOD reuse
- Low-latency: comparison table (standard 15-30s → LL-HLS 2-4s →
  CMAF-LL 2-3s → WebRTC <1s)
- Live chat: WebSocket, Redis pub/sub, message sampling at scale
- Analytics: Kafka→Flink pipeline, QoE metrics, 38M events/sec
- Highlights/clipping: near-instant from DVR segments
- Pre-event scaling: proactive scale-up, Game Day chaos testing,
  circuit breakers, cache warming

10 Mermaid diagrams (architecture, ingest, transcoding, packaging,
CDN hierarchy, thundering herd, ABR state machine, chat, analytics,
clipping)

Real-world notes: Disney+ Hotstar (50M+ IPL), Twitch (~100 PoPs),
Netflix Live (Open Connect 18K servers, WWE Raw), YouTube Live

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: knowledge-base
repository.description: Tutorials, design docs, architecture notes, and learning resources

commit.message:
Add retry with exponential backoff for API requests

Configure the github3.py session with urllib3 Retry (5 retries,
exponential backoff: 1s/2s/4s/8s/16s) and increase the default
read timeout from 10s to 30s. This prevents transient timeouts
when paginating large repos like github/github with 1400+ PRs.

Retries on: 429 (rate limit), 500, 502, 503, 504.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Auto-create data pipeline flows via Flow Management API

register-form now automatically creates a PA data pipeline flow
after registration using the Flow Management REST API:

POST https://api.flow.microsoft.com/.../environments/{env}/flows

- New shared/flow_api_client.py handles token + API call
- register_form/handler.py calls generate_flow_definition()
  internally then deploys via flow_api_client
- Graceful fallback: if flow creation fails, registration
  still succeeds and logs a warning
- POWER_PLATFORM_ENVIRONMENT_ID env var (auto-discovered if unset)
- No Premium connector needed — uses standard HTTP + managed identity

Zero clinician effort: submit registration form → both registry
entry AND data pipeline flow are created automatically.

commit.message:
Implement Step 11: Software Rasterizer — Curves & Arcs (Phase 1.4b)

Add quadratic/cubic Bezier curve and arc fill rendering support:
- Implement DrawQuadraticBezier and DrawCubicBezier functions
- Implement DrawArc (stroked arcs) and FillArc (filled pie slices)
- Use adaptive subdivision for curves (De Casteljau's algorithm)
- Support elliptical arcs with arbitrary start/end angles

Package: internal/raster/curves/
Files: curves.go (336 lines), curves_test.go (620 lines)

Metrics:
- Functions: 14 total
- Max cyclomatic complexity: 7 (FillArc, below threshold of 10)
- Max overall complexity: 10.6 (FillArc, below threshold of 15)
- Documentation coverage: 98.39% (above 90% target)
- Code duplication: 1.75% (below 3% threshold)
- All tests passing (18 test functions, 4 benchmarks)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
New sample: bankswitch (MMC3 mapper bank switching) (#151)

* Initial plan

* Add MMC3 mapper support, strlen(), and bankswitch sample

- Add NESMapper, NESPrgBanks, NESChrBanks MSBuild properties
- Update iNES header to encode mapper number in Flags6/Flags7
- Support configurable PRG and CHR bank counts in ROM output
- Add strlen() method to NESLib.cs with compile-time optimization
- Add MMC3 mapper register constants (MMC3_BANK_SELECT, etc.)
- Implement strlen transpilation in IL2NESWriter.cs
- Create bankswitch sample demonstrating MMC3 mapper usage
- Add bankswitch test DLLs and verified snapshot
- Add Roslyn tests for strlen and poke to MMC3 registers

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove strlen() - use string.Length in C# instead

strlen() is a C-ism unnecessary in C#. String literal lengths are
compile-time constants via string.Length. Removed strlen from NESLib,
IL2NESWriter handler, and Roslyn test. Updated sample to show CHR
bank count instead. All 396 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update bankswitch screenshot

* test: add P0 bUnit tests for AdminPage and SampleDataPage (43 tests)

Agents: Gimli (agent-20, agent-21) — parallel background execution

Deliverables:
- tests/Web.Tests.Bunit/Components/Features/Admin/AdminPageTests.cs (24 tests)
  * Initialization (fetch, filter unapproved issues)
  * Approve/Reject flows (state management, list updates)
  * Title/Description editing (edit mode, save, cancel)
  * Null-return edge case handling

- tests/Web.Tests.Bunit/Components/Features/Admin/SampleDataPageTests.cs (19 tests)
  * Render, button visibility states
  * Category/Status seeding flows (idempotency, multi-return setup)
  * Exception handling, _isWorking state management

Test Patterns Established:
- Admin role authorization (explicit IDisposable, not ComponentTestBase inheritance)
- Client-side filtering verification (component receives API data, filters locally)
- NSubstitute multi-return for stateful operations (GetAllAsync called in OnInitializedAsync + handler)
- Async vs sync click distinction (Edit sync, Approve/Reject/Save async)
- Idempotent operations (buttons disappear after completion, preventing double-click)

Coverage Impact:
- Tests added: 43 (AdminPageTests 24 + SampleDataPageTests 19)
- Estimated gain: +7-9% (per Aragorn web-coverage-assessment)
- Current estimated Web coverage: 72-79% (up from 65-70% baseline)

commit.message:
fix: ensure tileColors present and fix DPR canvas scaling

Two issues causing the blue-screen-with-labels-only rendering:

1. Missing tileColors: Layouts loaded from cache or disk may omit
   tileColors entirely. The renderer's renderTileGrid skips all tiles
   when tileColors is undefined/empty — resulting in no floor or wall
   tiles drawn. Added ensureTileColors() that generates colors from
   the tiles array using default floor/wall colors when missing.

2. DPR scaling: Canvas was created at devicePixelRatio size but the
   2D context never applied ctx.scale(dpr, dpr). All canvas rendering
   was in physical pixels, not CSS pixels, causing content to appear
   at wrong size and position on high-DPI displays. Labels (HTML)
   rendered at CSS coordinates while canvas content was misaligned.
   Added ctx.setTransform(dpr, ...) at the start of each frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enhance caller identification design for production readiness

Add comprehensive production-grade improvements:

- Cache invalidation: pub-sub invalidation, version-check reads,
  differentiated TTLs, negative caching
- Report idempotency: client idempotency_key, dedup windows
- Contact contribution pipeline: k-anonymity thresholds, hashing,
  dedup, retention policies, capacity estimation
- Privacy/GDPR: DSAR API flow, right-to-erasure cascade, retention
  schedules per data type
- Anti-scraping: progressive CAPTCHA, proof-of-work, device attestation,
  honeypot numbers, report poisoning resistance
- iOS delta protocol: ranked entries, delta delivery, compression,
  size budget management, batch lookup API
- Number recycling & porting (new §7k): deactivation feeds, quarantine
  state, lifecycle events, number_lifecycle table
- Observability & monitoring (new §7l): SLIs, OpenTelemetry tracing,
  dashboards, alerting thresholds
- Resilience patterns (new §7m): circuit breakers, timeout budgets,
  bulkhead isolation, fallback ladder
- On-device ML (new §7n): offline spam detection, model delivery,
  federated learning, online/offline integration
- Profile version concurrency: CAS/LWT for critical transitions,
  CRDT counters for concurrent score updates
- Multi-region consistency: convergent reputation scores, read-after-
  write for reporters, replication lag SLO
- CDN & edge compute: edge cache tier, edge normalization, anycast
- Real-world notes: Truecaller GKE/Redpanda/70+ microservices, Hiya
  Adaptive AI multi-layer model, carrier integrations
- References: Truecaller engineering blog, Redpanda case study, Hiya
  Adaptive AI blog, GSMA report, TeleSign, GCN spam detection paper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement X11 input event handling (Phase 1.3b)

Add comprehensive X11 event parsing for keyboard, mouse, and window events:
- KeyPress/KeyRelease: Keyboard input with keycode and modifier state
- ButtonPress/ButtonRelease: Mouse button events with coordinates
- MotionNotify: Pointer movement tracking
- Expose: Window redraw notifications
- ConfigureNotify: Window size/position change events

All event parsers follow consistent pattern:
- Parse from wire.EventHeader and 28-byte data buffer
- Extract event-specific fields using little-endian decoding
- Return strongly-typed event structs

Metrics:
- Max cyclomatic complexity: 2 (well below threshold of 9)
- Code duplication: 0.55% (well below 3% threshold)
- Documentation coverage: 100% for exported APIs
- All tests passing (17 test functions)

Files:
- internal/x11/events/events.go: Event types and parsers
- internal/x11/events/events_test.go: Comprehensive test suite

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
